prompt: Create a wide cinematic photograph of a minimalist contemporary installation artwork in a white gallery room. The scene shows dozens of tiny suspended metal, acrylic, wire, and glass fragments hanging from the ceiling by thin vertical strings, carefully arranged in midair like an exploded deconstructed sculpture. Count the visible suspended components as about 45 discrete pieces: thin rods, small rectangular plates, angled strips, circular ring, crossbars, and tiny connector blocks, all in pale silver, clear, and off-white materials. A strong soft spotlight from above and behind projects their combined shadows onto the smooth concrete floor, forming four huge blurred Chinese characters reading 来都来了. The shadow typography should dominate the foreground, oversized and soft-edged, stretching toward the viewer with realistic perspective. The physical hanging fragments should appear abstract and unreadable by themselves, but their shadows clearly align into the text. Use a serene museum aesthetic, warm white walls, curved seamless floor-to-wall transition, subtle haze, high-key lighting, long soft shadows, lots of negative space, and a calm beige-gray palette. Add very small exhibition-style captions near the bottom corners: on the lower left place the Chinese title 来都来了 with a tiny English subtitle beneath it reading "COME SINCE YOU ARE HERE"; on the lower right add tiny bilingual-looking museum metadata lines including BRAND INSTALLATION, "2024", and "SITE SPECIFIC". Keep the typography on the floor as shadow only, not printed ink. Photorealistic architectural installation art, 16:9 aspect ratio, viewed from a low frontal angle, no people, no clutter, no watermark.
